steps.title: Recognize the two-part task
steps.content: The student must both state a general principle about comet orbits and provide specific evidence that proves it—not merely pair two unrelated facts.

steps.title: Locate the generalization and its supporting example
steps.content:
D opens with a generalization (“A comet's orbit around the Sun may change over time”) and immediately backs it with 81P/Wild as evidence—its orbit shifted from between Uranus and Jupiter to between Jupiter and Mars, proving the claim.

  • A provides a generalization without a specific example
  • C presents an example without stating the generalization
  • B misrepresents the planetary data
.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
